Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Hmm this is a structured process analysis task with clear normative rules and desiderata The user wants me to analyze three loan application cases against these criteria Let me break it down step by step

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to carefully review the normative constraints The key is the strict sequence Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Inform Applicant Finalize Documentation if approved Close Case Any deviation here is a serious violation Then there are desiderata like timing recommendations and follow up calls important but not critical

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at Case 1 it follows the sequence perfectly Credit Check is before Risk Assessment and after approval documentation is finalized before closing Also timing is good Credit Check within half an hour applicant informed the same day No follow up call but thats optional So Case 1 is clean

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 has two clear normative violations Risk Assessment happened before Credit Check and documentation was finalized after rejection Both break the mandatory sequence The timing is okay but the order errors are serious No follow up call here either

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 is interesting The sequence is correct Credit Check before Risk Assessment etc But the timing is poor Credit Check after 48 hours and applicant informed two days late These breach the desiderata not the rules Also no follow up call So its suboptimal but not invalid

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I should structure my response to first address normative violations then desiderata and finally offer interpretations where possible For Case 2 I can speculate about reasons like system errors or human mistakes but the violations stand For Case 3 the delays might be due to backlog or resource issues but theyre still not ideal
